title: CAT'S CLAW
description: Cat's Claw (Uncaria tomentosa) is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest known for its potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. Its mechanisms include the inhibition of NF-kappaB and the reduction of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-6, making it a primary supplement for managing chronic inflammatory conditions and supporting immune health.

research.title: Anti-inflammatory and/or immunomodulatory activities of Uncaria tomentosa (cat's claw) extracts: A systematic review and meta-analysis of in vivo studies
research.summary: This meta-analysis confirmed that Cat's Claw significantly reduces pro-inflammatory mediators like IL-6 and NF-κB, validating its traditional use for inflammatory diseases.

research.title: Uncaria tomentosa (Cat's Claw) Improves Quality of Life in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors
research.summary: Research indicates that Cat's Claw supplementation significantly reduces fatigue and improves the overall quality of life and social functioning in patients with advanced cancer.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view Cat's Claw as a powerful tool for 'dragging oneself out' of states of severe fatigue and chronic illness, frequently citing its effectiveness in complex recovery stacks.

effects.label: Quality of Life:
effects.description: Clinical evidence shows it improves social functioning and reduces the 'burden' of chronic disease symptoms.

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Users report high effectiveness when paired with lifestyle changes like exercise and specific dietary protocols.

effects.label: Symptom Management:
effects.description: While not always 'curing' underlying conditions, it is highly effective at managing secondary symptoms like brain fog and lethargy.

dosage.label: Standard Extract Dose:
dosage.description: Many clinical studies utilize 100mg of dry extract taken three times daily.

dosage.label: Biohacker Consensus:
dosage.description: Users often start with lower doses to assess tolerance before moving to standard therapeutic ranges.

dosage.label: Tea vs. Capsules:
dosage.description: Traditional use favors bark tea, but supplement users prefer standardized extracts for consistent alkaloid content.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Sensitivity:
sideEffects.description: Some users with sensitive stomachs report mild digestive upset or nausea.

sideEffects.label: Blood Pressure Interaction:
sideEffects.description: Potential for hypotensive effects, requiring caution when combined with other blood-pressure-lowering agents.

sideEffects.label: Hormonal Influence:
sideEffects.description: Minimal anecdotal reports suggest monitoring mood changes during specific hormonal cycles (e.g., PMDD).

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter dietary supplement in most countries.

availability.label: Sourcing Varieties:
availability.description: Critical to distinguish between Uncaria tomentosa (preferred) and Uncaria guaianensis, as their chemical profiles differ.
